articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.name: Messi celeb Arg Iceland_260609 [1296x729]
images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.
images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

article.headline: Germany salvages draw with Ukraine in charity friendly
article.description: Kai Havertz helped Germany salvage a 3-3 draw with Ukraine in a charity friendly that left neither team happy from a sporting perspective on Monday.
article.linkText: Germany draws with Ukraine in charity friendly

article.story:
Kai Havertz helped Germany salvage a 3-3 draw with Ukraine in a charity friendly that left neither team happy from a sporting perspective on Monday.

Viktor Tsyhankov had scored twice for Ukraine to come from behind and lead 3-1 in Serhiy Rebrov's debut as coach, putting his team on course for the country's first-ever win over Germany.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

But Havertz pulled one back in the 83rd and was then fouled for a penalty that Joshua Kimmich converted in stoppage time for the equalizer.

The German soccer federation was donating the proceeds to victims of Russia's war in Ukraine. The match was also billed as the federation's 1,000th, but the team's performance will do little to restore battered German confidence a year before the country hosts the European Championship.

The buildup was overshadowed by the war in Ukraine with both teams taking to the field accompanied by children who had fled their country to escape the invasion.

Every visiting player was draped in a Ukrainian flag. The teams posed for individual photos -- with Ukraine's in front of another giant blue-and-yellow flag -- before German and Ukrainian players stood for a mixed photo behind a banner with the word "peace."

The German players also had a peace symbol on the arms of their jerseys.

Niclas Fullkrug, playing in his home stadium, was gifted a huge chance by Mykhailo Mudryk in the second minute but the Werder Bremen forward missed with only the goalkeeper to beat.

Fullkrug was credited with the opening goal in the sixth when he deflected Marius Wolf's shot past Anatoliy Trubin in the visitors' goal.

Germany missed further good chances against the disorganized visitors before Tsyhankov equalized on a counter-attack in the 19th.

Mudryk forced an own goal from Antonio Rüdiger four minutes later to make it 2-1, delighting the visiting supporters. The Ukrainian ambassador to Germany, Oleksii Makeiev, celebrated with restraint beside German president Frank-Walter Steinmeier, who smiled and held out his hand to congratulate him.

Buoyed by the goals, Ukraine pushed for more before the home team recovered.

But it was a typical display of late from Germany -- seemingly in control, creating chances, yet still prone to costly mistakes.

Coach Hansi Flick reacted by bringing on Havertz and Lukas Klostermann for the second half, but little changed.

Artem Dovbyk capitalized on another mistake to set up Tsyhankov for the third goal in the 56th.

Further German mistakes led to whistles from some in the crowd, but they were finally given reason to cheer when Rüdiger sent a long ball forward for Havertz to score.

Havertz was then fouled as he pushed for the equalizer and Kimmich, the Germany captain, made no mistake from the spot.

Goal!  Germany 1, Ukraine 1. Viktor Tsygankov (Ukraine) right footed shot from the right side of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Oleksandr Tymchyk with a through ball   following a fast break.Goal awarded following VAR Review.
Own Goal by Antonio Rüdiger, Germany.  Germany 1, Ukraine 2.
First Half ends, Germany 1, Ukraine 2.
Second Half begins Germany 1, Ukraine 2.
Substitution, Germany. Kai Havertz replaces Niclas Füllkrug.
Substitution, Germany. Lukas Klostermann replaces Nico Schlotterbeck.

Substitution, Germany. Jamal Musiala replaces Leon Goretzka.
Substitution, Germany. Jonas Hofmann replaces Marius Wolf.
Substitution, Ukraine. Vladyslav Vanat replaces Artem Dovbyk.

Ruslan Malinovskyi (Ukraine) is shown the yellow card.
Goal!  Germany 3, Ukraine 3. Joshua Kimmich (Germany) converts the penalty with a right footed shot to the bottom left corner.
Second Half ends, Germany 3, Ukraine 3.
